_meta.authored_from: §9 Resolution A + sourcePrecedence.ts (local-first precedence resolver at grant endpoint)
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.reviewed_by: ambitions-audit synthesis (recommender-api-shape: no available_on in ScoredItem yet; media-core owns availability join — Path A)
_meta.purpose: Cross-language ordering invariant for the available-source precedence resolver. The grant endpoint asks the recommender for ranked tmdb_ids, then joins each id to availability via (a) iptv_title_link (in iptv.db), (b) media_files (in media.db, future M3), (c) Plex /identity probe (in plex.ts). The order in which sources are tried and the final `available_on: string[]` shape are contract-stable.

precedence_rationale: local: Direct-play from media-core (M3+) is always cheapest — no transcode, no upstream cap, no third-party.
plex: Already-Plex-shared content is the user's existing default play path. Higher than iptv because Plex transcodes are local to the household.
iptv: External upstream (mybunny.tv concurrency cap applies). Cheapest re-discovery path when local + plex both miss.

available_on_shape.ordering: MUST match precedence_order — local first, plex second, iptv third. Consumers (UI badge renderer, Apple source picker) rely on this ordering to surface the recommended option visually first.

vectors.name: tmdb-only
vectors.scenario: Recommender returns a tmdb_id; no source has it.

expected.comment: No available_on entries → suggestion is filtered from the user-facing list. Don't surface dead recommendations.

vectors.name: iptv-only-active
vectors.scenario: Recommender returns a tmdb_id; only iptv_title_link has an active row.

vectors.name: iptv-tombstoned
vectors.scenario: iptv_title_link row exists but is tombstoned (removed_at is set).

expected.comment: Tombstoned rows MUST be filtered. Partial index iptv_link_active_by_tmdb (WHERE removed_at IS NULL) is the query path.

vectors.name: iptv-plus-plex
vectors.scenario: Both iptv and Plex have the title.

expected.comment: plex precedes iptv per the precedence_order. UI shows 'Play on Plex' as the primary CTA, 'Play via IPTV' as the secondary.

vectors.name: all-three
vectors.scenario: Future M3+ scenario — local file, plex, and iptv all have the title.

expected.comment: Local always wins. UI shows 'Play (local)' as primary.
Same precedence rules apply across kinds. Per §9, iptv_kind 'series' maps to tmdb_kind 'tv'; iptv_kind 'vod' maps to 'movie'.

negative_checks.name: no-duplicates
negative_checks.assertion: available_on MUST NOT contain duplicate values. Each source appears at most once.

negative_checks.name: case-sensitive
negative_checks.assertion: Values are lowercase ASCII. 'Plex', 'IPTV', 'LOCAL' are all invalid — consumers SHOULD reject them rather than silently coerce.

negative_checks.name: stable-ordering
negative_checks.assertion: Two calls to the precedence resolver with identical input MUST produce identical available_on arrays in identical order. No set iteration ordering allowed.
